items.title: Causal contributions of left inferior and medial frontal cortex to semantic and executive control
items.author: Gesa Hartwigsen
items.content: Perturbing medial and left inferior frontal cortices with TMS differentiates semantic from domain-general executive control, with compensatory interactions revealing a flexible, interconnected cognitive control network.

items.title: Lead binds HIF-1α contributing to depression-like behaviour through modulating mitochondria-associated astrocyte ferroptosis
items.author: Yanshu Zhang
items.content: Lead exposure induces depression-like behaviour in mice via astrocyte ferroptosis. Pb binds HIF-1α, preventing degradation and promoting VDAC1- mediated mitochondrial dysfunction, revealing a novel HIF-1α/VDAC1 axis in Pb neurotoxicity.

items.title: Digging into the evolutionary history of the fungus-growing-ant symbiont, Escovopsis (Hypocreaceae)
items.author: Andre Rodrigues
items.content: Comprehensive analysis of Escovopsis fungi across the Americas traces its evolution, distribution, and adaptations, providing insights into their complex symbiotic relationship with ants.

items.title: Anatomical circuits for flexible spatial mapping by single neurons in posterior parietal cortex
items.author: Kristine Krug
items.content: Neuronal tracing reveals unbalanced circuits between two distinct operational compartments in primate lateral intraparietal area (LIP), which underpin single LIP neurons with spatially different sensory and motor receptive fields mapped neurophysiologically.

items.title: Neddylation regulates the development and function of glutamatergic neurons
items.author: Marilyn Tirard
items.content: This study highlights a pivotal role for Neddylation in controlling the fate of glutamatergic neurons and excitatory synaptic transmission.

items.title: Contrasting contribution of resident and repopulated brain macrophages in sustaining sleep-wake circuitry
items.author: Gianni Monaco
items.content: Brain-resident macrophages regulate sleep; their depletion disrupts sleep patterns, synaptic and neurochemical balance. Repopulation partially restores sleep but induces fragmentation and synaptic imbalance.

items.title: Mitochondria delay action potential propagation
items.author: Pepe Alcami
items.content: Modeling mitochondria-containing thin unmyelinated axons based on songbird premotor axon electron microscopy shows that mitochondria slow conduction velocity, delaying action potential propagation.

items.title: Author Correction: Rapid modulation in music supports attention in listeners with attentional difficulties
items.author: Psyche Loui
items.content: Author Correction: Rapid modulation in music supports attention in listeners with attentional difficulties
